channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Gas crosses $4 a gallon in U.S. for the first time in 3 years
item.description: The war with Iran has driven up gas prices at a time when affordability is high on people's minds.
item.content:encoded:
The war with Iran has driven up gas prices at a time when affordability is high on people's minds.

(Image credit: Ronaldo Schemidt)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: 6 books named finalists for the 2026 International Booker Prize
item.description: The shortlisted titles include novels and novellas from authors and translators spanning four continents, with stories that range from Japanese-controlled 1930s Taiwan to the streets of Tehran in 1979.
item.content:encoded: The shortlisted titles include novels and novellas from authors and translators spanning four continents, with stories that range from Japanese-controlled 1930s Taiwan to the streets of Tehran in 1979.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: DHS resumes asylum decisions. And, Iran's strike injures over 12 U.S. personnel
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security has lifted its ban on reviewing asylum applications. And, NPR has confirmed that an Iranian strike injured over a dozen U.S. personnel.
item.content:encoded:
The Department of Homeland Security has lifted its ban on reviewing asylum applications. And, NPR has confirmed that an Iranian strike injured over a dozen U.S. personnel.

(Image credit: Herika Martinez)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Trump tells Europe 'get your own oil,' Iran hits oil tanker off Dubai
item.description: Iran attacked and set on fire a massive Kuwaiti oil tanker off Dubai overnight, as Gulf states increasingly suffer the fallout from the war.
item.content:encoded:
Iran attacked and set on fire a massive Kuwaiti oil tanker off Dubai overnight, as Gulf states increasingly suffer the fallout from the war.

(Image credit: Majid Saeedi)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: In a town close to the farmworker movement, some struggle to process Chavez allegations
item.description: March 31 is Cesar Chavez's birthday, and a longtime holiday. In the wake of sexual assault allegations against him, residents in the farming town of Delano are conflicted about how to remember him.
item.content:encoded:
March 31 is Cesar Chavez's birthday, and a longtime holiday. In the wake of sexual assault allegations against him, residents in the farming town of Delano are conflicted about how to remember him.

(Image credit: Jennifer Emerling for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Kerry Klein

item.title: The final batch of World Cup tickets is about to go on sale. Here are 5 things to know
item.description: FIFA is kicking off its last sales for World Cup tickets on Wednesday. From prices to why FOMO is working against you, here's what you need to know.
item.content:encoded:
FIFA is kicking off its last sales for World Cup tickets on Wednesday. From prices to why FOMO is working against you, here's what you need to know.

(Image credit: Robert Cianflone)
item.dc:creator: Rafael Nam

item.title: How Trump's EEOC is attacking DEI and emphasizing white people
item.description: Andrea Lucas, the Trump-appointed chair of the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission, has set a new agenda for an agency that long prioritized vulnerable and underserved workers.
item.content:encoded:
Andrea Lucas, the Trump-appointed chair of the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission, has set a new agenda for an agency that long prioritized vulnerable and underserved workers.

(Image credit: Elizabeth Gillis)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: Babies are an afterthought in the birthright citizenship case, advocates say
item.description: An end to birthright citizenship would mean a new layer of bureaucracy for all babies born in the U.S. and could cause delays for health insurance and other benefits.
item.content:encoded:
An end to birthright citizenship would mean a new layer of bureaucracy for all babies born in the U.S. and could cause delays for health insurance and other benefits.

(Image credit: Drew Angerer)
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Iran opens Strait of Hormuz to some ships as Gulf countries face strikes, Trump's mixed messages on the war in Iran could hurt the GOP, TSA officers are receiving back pay as DHS remains unfunded.
item.content:encoded: Iran opens Strait of Hormuz to some ships as Gulf countries face strikes, Trump's mixed messages on the war in Iran could hurt the GOP, TSA officers are receiving back pay as DHS remains unfunded.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: 'We call it the walking bus': How kids are getting to school amid ICE operations
item.description: In cities where ICE operations have surged, community members are walking kids to school.
item.content:encoded:
In cities where ICE operations have surged, community members are walking kids to school.

(Image credit: LA Johnson)
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel
